title: Prevent disk saturation from schedule node
symptom: A schedule trigger node set to a short interval can cause excessive database growth, leading to disk saturation and potential data loss.
applies_when: Set to a short interval (e.g., 5 seconds)
not_this_if: Trigger interval is set to 1 minute or longer
root_cause: When a schedule node is set to trigger too frequently, it can generate excessive data in the database, leading to rapid disk space consumption. This can result in the inability to store new data, causing system failures.
fix_steps: Adjust the schedule node interval to at least 1 minute.
Enable automatic data pruning by adding specific environment variables to docker-compose.yml.
Monitor disk space regularly using a cron job.
Implement a backup strategy for the SQLite database.
agent_instruction: Adjust the schedule node interval and enable data pruning in your configuration.
